title: The 50/30/20 Rule
summary: A simple split for every dollar you make.
doThisToday: Take whatever cash you have right now and mentally split it 50/30/20. See how it feels.

cards.prompt: Once you can spot needs vs. wants, you need a plan for how much goes where.
cards.explanation: That's what the 50/30/20 rule is for — a fast way to divide up any money you earn.
50% of your money covers needs, 30% covers wants, and 20% goes to savings or paying off debt. It's a starting point, not a law.

cards.prompt: You made $300 this month. Drag the sliders to build the 50/30/20 split yourself.
cards.explanation: 50% ($150) to needs, 30% ($90) to wants, 20% ($60) to savings. Once you can build this split yourself, you can apply it to any amount of money you ever earn.

cards.prompt: Quick check: using 50/30/20, how much of a $500 paycheck should go toward needs?
cards.explanation: 50% of $500 is $250 — same math, different number.

cards.prompt: You want to save up for a car in 8 months. Which part of the 50/30/20 split should you try to grow?
cards.explanation: Big goals get funded by the savings slice. You might even flex it up to 30-40% temporarily if the goal matters enough.

cards.prompt: True or false: the 50/30/20 rule means you must split your money exactly that way every single month.
cards.explanation: It's a guideline, not a law. Some months needs run higher, some months you push savings harder for a goal. The point is making sure needs and savings get covered before wants eat the whole paycheck.

cards.prompt: The 50/30/20 rule has a well-known origin.
cards.frontText: Who popularized the 50/30/20 rule in a bestselling personal finance book?
cards.backText: Senator Elizabeth Warren, in the 2005 book "All Your Worth," co-written with her daughter Amelia Warren Tyagi — before her political career, she was a bankruptcy law professor who studied why families ran out of money.
cards.explanation: It's stuck around for two decades because it's simple enough to actually use, which matters more than being perfectly precise.

cards.prompt: 50/30/20 isn't magic — it's a fast gut-check.
cards.explanation: If your wants are eating up more than 30% of your money most months, your savings is almost always what pays the price.
